titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 580

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=902 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.617886+00 | translation moa=903 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.629286+00

p.cl:
M . Rex Scott’ . Iusticiis . vicecomitibus . Prepositis . Ministris . Cunctisque aliis probis hominibus totius
terre sue tam futuris quam presentibus salutem .

Sciatis me concessisse deo et Sancto Cuthberto de Dunelmo
et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus  ut ipsi et eorum seruientibus per totam terram meam sint liberi et
quieti ab omni teloneo de propriis catallis et mercaturis eorum . Prohibeo itaque ut nemo contra hanc libertatem
eis infra terram meam uim aut iniuria faciat super forisfacturam meam .

Testibus Nicol’ Camerario . Ricardo Capellano
Regis . Ricardo de Sancto Albano clerico regis .
Malcolm, king of the Scots, to the justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all other worthy men of his entire land, as much future as present, greeting.

Know me to have conceded to God, Saint Cuthbert of Durham, and to the monks serving God in that place, that they and their tenants throughout my entire land be free and quit from every toll on their own cattle and merchandise. I therefore forbid that anyone do violence or injury to them contrary to this liberty within my land upon pain of my forfeiture.
As witnesses: Nicholas the chamberlain, Richard the king's chaplain, Richard of St Albans the king's clerk.
